123250.explanation: The executive board must have a balanced composition that reflects society and does justice to the voters' voice. Primarily, all members of the executive must have the right competencies suitable for their position and portfolio within the board.

123251.explanation: Participation by residents increases support for policies. Taking part in a referendum is a form of resident participation. For a successful referendum, the rules must be clear from the outset and it must be clear what the executive board and municipal council will do with the results.

123254.explanation: Allowing a campsite or shop to open at every farm is not possible. That disrupts the balance between tourism and livability for our residents. However, the municipality must cooperate with farmers regarding possibilities for New Economic Drivers. The starting point: What is possible!

123257.explanation: In Zierikzee, an emergency shelter will open for a maximum of 250 people for up to 5 years. We will not cooperate with expanding or extending the emergency shelter. We are and remain opposed to establishing a regular asylum seekers' center on our island.

123258.explanation: Housing for seasonal or temporary workers is a shared responsibility of employers in, for example, the hospitality and recreation sector, the agriculture and horticulture sectors, and the municipality. The initiative lies with the entrepreneurs, and housing on their own company grounds is preferred.

123262.explanation: Local initiatives from our communities to organize an event on New Year's Eve should be encouraged and facilitated by the municipality within the event policy. A fireworks show or carbide shooting can be part of that.

123263.explanation: We have an excellent recycling center where waste/bulky waste is collected separately. Each household has its own card which entitles them to bring bulky waste to the recycling center free of charge.

123266.explanation: If a farmer has land adjacent to a community and has a good plan for housing that fits within our housing policy, then that should be possible.

123267.explanation: To stay healthy and vital, it is important that all our residents can participate in sports and exercise. Membership of a sports club should therefore be included in the minimum provisions for residents with a low income.

123270.explanation: Through the Joint Arrangement of the Zeeland Music School, we as a municipality invest in music education. Paying for music lessons is primarily the responsibility of the parents. Parents who cannot afford this financially should be supported so their children can still take music lessons.

123274.explanation: The safety of our residents and guests is important. There is relatively little police presence on our island. Having enough BOA officers focused on safety is therefore necessary and partly compensates for the shortage of deployable police capacity.

123275.explanation: Camera surveillance for the sake of camera surveillance cannot be the purpose. More camera surveillance is not automatically needed. Camera surveillance must be proportional. Where necessary, (additional) camera surveillance should be deployed to help prevent insecurity, violence, or (structural) vandalism.

123276.explanation: Schouwen-Duiveland is too small to independently organize acute 24-hour crisis shelters such as a women's shelter. Together with all Zeeland municipalities, we have provincially organized all forms of acute 24-hour (crisis) shelters, day and night shelters, homeless care, and women's crisis shelters.

123280.explanation: Holiday homes in vacation parks are not suitable for permanent residence. Tourists simply have a different lifestyle rhythm than residents. Mixing those target groups causes nuisance. It is better to focus on ensuring that all homes in communities with a residential designation are truly permanently inhabited.

123283.explanation: Municipal taxes are used for facilities for our residents. The principle is that residents must simply pay taxes. Therefore, no generic waiver for residents with a low income. However, tailor-made solutions should be provided for residents who truly cannot afford to pay!

123284.explanation: Financial self-reliance is a condition for participating in society. Financial self-reliance does not depend solely on income. Residents who, for whatever reason, are unable to be financially self-reliant should be able to receive support to achieve this.

123287.explanation: The maximum speed depends on the local situation. 50 km/h is the standard within built-up areas. Road design should match the applicable speed limit so that keeping to the speed is logical for road users. Home zones/neighborhoods are suitable for a 30 km/h regime.

123288.explanation: There is a growing shortage of well-qualified care workers. Care has nothing to do with religion, culture, or beliefs. The important thing is that residents receive the right care on time and as close as possible.

123289.explanation: Our youth must be able to receive the (youth) help they need to grow up safely into self-reliant adults. Price must not be leading in selecting youth care organizations; quality and effectiveness of the help must be paramount.

123290.explanation: Property tax is necessary to maintain our level of facilities.

123292.explanation: Through the tourist tax, our guests contribute to the infrastructure and level of facilities on our island. The tourist tax is used, among other things, for urban and village renewal. Therefore, the tourist tax must increase annually by at least the inflation rate.

123294.explanation: Apply custom solutions per community. In those communities where there is support, Sunday shopping should simply be allowed.

123295.explanation: The municipality's primary responsibility lies in realizing sufficient social rental housing for our own residents.

123297.explanation: Solar panels on roofs are fine, but no solar panels on agricultural land. Agricultural land is meant for growing food, and that must remain so.

123298.explanation: Pop venue Brogum is a broad cultural facility mainly aimed at our youth. As a municipality, we want to be attractive for young people to live, thrive, and build their future here. Then we must also be willing to invest in (cultural) facilities for our youth.

123304.explanation: Renting a room by the room can be a first step towards independent living for young starters.

123305.explanation: Our residents must be able to park affordably in the communities. We have a parking permit to make this possible. We can use that parking permit to make parking more expensive for day tourists.

123307.explanation: The center of Zierikzee is already low-traffic; that is sufficient.

123308.explanation: We already have a sustainability loan and (local) sustainability subsidies.

123310.explanation: As a municipality, we do not have authority over parking facilities along the Brouwersdam.

123250.explanation: An equal distribution fits as a reflection of society.

123251.explanation: It is good for residents to be able to give their opinion on important subjects. There are also more instruments for this, such as 'participate think along', through online consultations.

123254.explanation: It is good if farmers can sell directly to citizens with a shop without intermediaries. We are very cautious regarding the realization of new campsites.

123257.explanation: We have an emergency shelter with clear agreements. It is important that we adhere to these agreements.

123258.explanation: Yes, as far as possible and provided that the facilities are adequate.

123262.explanation: The municipality should encourage activities on New Year's Eve, but preferably a drone show instead of a fireworks display. Be original for once!

123263.explanation: People are used to taking it themselves, and that works perfectly fine.

123266.explanation: The countryside should not become too built-up. There is more of a pull towards the city than towards the countryside. Adding more houses in rural areas may eventually lead to vacancies. In principle, we build within the cores on our island.

123267.explanation: We already have that. There are various facilities available for people with social assistance benefits, and that should be maintained.

123270.explanation: Music is a great asset and quite expensive. Therefore, it is important that the municipality contributes to it. It is also good if music education is provided through school.

123274.explanation: As far as is known, there are no capacity issues. We have solved those earlier.

123275.explanation: There is no reason to increase camera surveillance to more locations.

123276.explanation: There are various facilities available in this area on the island and in the region.

123280.explanation: Holiday homes are primarily for vacation purposes. In case of emergency, such as a divorce, it should be possible to live temporarily in a recreational home.

123283.explanation: If people cannot afford it, the municipality should be accommodating in this matter.

123284.explanation: In recent years we have already raised the income threshold, and we want to keep it that way.

123287.explanation: Everywhere within the built-up area is a fine principle. The built-up area should be for pedestrians and cyclists. Motorized traffic must drive slowly through a village or town center.

123288.explanation: A healthcare provider should not concern themselves with religion, culture, or belief; they must be there for everyone.

123289.explanation: The municipality must pay attention to the price-quality ratio. Primarily, care must be good; after that, it is good to look at the price. But quality comes first.

123290.explanation: There is no reason to lower property tax (OZB). Generally, people can meet these financial obligations well.

123292.explanation: At this moment there is no reason to raise the tourist tax. An annual indexation is sufficient at this time.

123294.explanation: Sunday rest is highly valued in certain villages. It is good not to organize shopping Sundays there. In other centers, having shopping Sundays is fine.

123295.explanation: People with residence permits must leave the asylum seeker centers because these are overcrowded. Therefore, it is good that appropriate numbers of recognized refugees are given priority. By the way, this number is limited.

123297.explanation: Prohibiting is too strong, but it is low on the sustainability ladder: first apply other options before using fertile agricultural land for this purpose.

123298.explanation: Culture costs money. Therefore, it is important that pop venue Brogum continues to receive financial support.

123304.explanation: That is possible, for example in the form of a landlady renting out a room. We would like to see such a construction realized on our island.

123305.explanation: There is no reason to make paid parking more expensive in the centers.

123307.explanation: Fine to further reduce car use in the center of Zierikzee and only allow it for residents and people who must be there for deliveries, visiting family/friends, and the like.

123308.explanation: We must keep subsidies in this context, such as for applying insulation measures: insulating roofs, crawl spaces, walls, double glazing, etc.

123310.explanation: It currently costs campers nothing, although we do need to maintain the infrastructure, etc. It's fine if they have to pay something.

123250.explanation: Ideally yes, of course, but it also depends on availability. Quality always takes precedence.

123251.explanation: Complicated decision-making often cannot be captured in a simple 'for' or 'against'.

123254.explanation: Yes, but under conditions; the municipality must always take a positive attitude where a farm wants to generate extra income. But certainly not always. It depends on the nature of the activities and the extent to which they apply.

123257.explanation: Schouwen-Duiveland wants to do its fair share in solving the asylum reception crisis in the Netherlands. Therefore, the CDA is also in favor of maintaining the distribution law.

123258.explanation: Primarily, the responsibility for housing seasonal workers lies with employers. Where it is realistic and possible, the municipality should create the right conditions for this.

123262.explanation: This is not the responsibility of the municipality. However, it can support the possible organization of this.

123263.explanation: It is in the public interest. For example, it prevents a lot of litter.

123266.explanation: No more or less than is already the case through current regulations. In-filling rather than expansion.

123267.explanation: There are currently sufficient existing provisions such as the Sports Fund. If these prove insufficient, the municipality does have a role. But only then.

123270.explanation: Music education makes a substantial contribution to multiple aspects of a child's development. Lack of subsidy would mean that groups of children could not participate in this form of education. This is undesirable.

123275.explanation: There are certainly places where there is added value, for example from a preventive perspective.

123276.explanation: As far as we know, there is no need for this in S-D. It is fine to handle this in a regional context.

123287.explanation: This is already the case almost everywhere

123288.explanation: Always goes too far. But the starting point should be that it is possible.

123294.explanation: We prefer to maintain the current situation where shops are allowed to be open in most centers

123295.explanation: We are lagging behind on the targets. A priority scheme is necessary to deliver our fair share

123298.explanation: Of course Brogum has the right to subsidies, but proportionally

123308.explanation: We struggle with this. Primarily the responsibility of the owner.

123310.explanation: As long as it (is) possible it is a nice exception to the rule in coastal municipalities

123250.explanation: Disagree: Diversity is important and provides a broader perspective and a stronger team. We choose talent and competence over imposed distribution. Balance is good, as long as quality is not compromised.

123251.explanation: Agree: Residents must have input on major decisions. This can be through village councils or various other forms of participation. That way we ensure policies align with what is happening in our villages and cities.

123254.explanation: Agree: Farmers must be given space to operate and become future-proof. Innovation and side activities such as a shop or mini-campsite are welcome, provided they fit policy and environment. Think in terms of possibilities, not limitations.

123257.explanation: Agree: Asylum reception must close as soon as possible. Due to the Distribution Law, we had to choose: permanent AZC or temporary emergency reception. VVD chose emergency reception, max 5 years, and voted against 10 years. Intake must decrease. A deal is a deal! Listen: https://tinyurl.com/HetBlikkenTorentje

123258.explanation: Agree: Housing for seasonal workers can be on or near the company if it is safe, clean, and legal, with attention to living quality and surroundings. Housing may also be outside the company, provided no uncontrolled growth or mini-village arises.

123262.explanation: Agree: If private fireworks are banned, a municipal fireworks show can be a safe alternative to preserve tradition and limit nuisance. We prioritize supporting initiatives from the villages and cities.

123263.explanation: Disagree: We don’t want waste on the street. That’s why there are recycling sites with passes and clear explanations. This allows us to separate and monitor waste well. For seniors and vulnerable people, we make arrangements: collection is possible but against payment; otherwise, it becomes too expensive for everyone.

123266.explanation: Agree: It must be possible, under conditions, to build on one’s own property! Think of informal care, pre-informal care homes, or to ensure succession. Housing on agricultural land is desirable if it fits within the landscape and living environment.

123267.explanation: Agree: Sports are healthy and help people participate. The municipality can lower barriers via existing schemes such as the Declaration Fund (€175), the Adult Fund, and the Youth Fund. This stimulates participation without structural costs.

123270.explanation: Agree: Music encourages talent and development. We find it important children get this chance, especially if parents cannot afford it. Support via existing funds is better than a new general subsidy.

123274.explanation: Agree: The sense of safety is under pressure due to rising risks such as nuisance and crime. More public enforcement officers in the neighborhood provide visibility, quick problem resolution, and prevention. This gives residents, especially seniors and vulnerable groups, more confidence and peace.

123275.explanation: Agree: VVD wants more camera surveillance in places with rising risks like nuisance and crime. Cameras help with detection, prevention, and increase the sense of security. They support enforcement officers and police, so problems are addressed faster.

123276.explanation: Agree: VVD wants victims of domestic violence to receive quick and safe shelter. A women’s shelter offers protection and help, reduces risks, and gives residents, especially vulnerable groups such as women and seniors, a safe way out.

123280.explanation: Agree: VVD wants more small rental and owner-occupied homes for seniors, starters, and singles. This enables them to live independently in their familiar environment. This increases housing market flow and creates space for returnees and families.

123283.explanation: Agree: VVD supports tailored solutions, we help those who truly cannot pay. But waivers should not be an incentive to avoid work. Work must pay and everyone contributes where possible.

123284.explanation: Disagree: We do not want automatic expansion. Help must be targeted at those who truly need it, not at increasing dependency. Work must pay and personal responsibility remains important.

123287.explanation: Disagree: Not everywhere 30 km/h. That slows traffic on safe, wide roads. We want tailor-made solutions: 30 km/h where necessary for safety, but 50 km/h on main roads for flow and accessibility.

123288.explanation: Agree: Freedom of choice is important. People should be able to decide who helps them, as long as this fits within rules and budgets. Self-direction and appropriate care come first.

123289.explanation: Disagree: In youth care quality counts above all. Cheap care that does not help leads later to more additional care. We choose effective support that truly helps young people move forward.

123290.explanation: Agree: We do not want increases and where possible aim for lower burdens for residents. This can be achieved by first cutting in our own organization and tackling waste. Being economical with taxpayers’ money is paramount.

123292.explanation: Disagree: We only apply inflation correction. VAT on overnight stays already rose from 9% to 21%, a significant increase. Raising it further harms tourism, our economic engine. Tourist money must largely flow back into beautification of the centers.

123294.explanation: Disagree: Entrepreneurs must have freedom to open. More shopping Sundays give residents choices and stimulate the local economy. The municipality should not restrict unnecessarily.

123295.explanation: Disagree: There is a major housing shortage. VVD wants priority for our own residents and returnees. A new law offers the possibility to arrange priority for local residents, but our motion to arrange this received no support from the college and coalition.

123297.explanation: Agree: Agricultural land is for food production, not solar panels. VVD wants to use roofs and business parks first. Only for compelling reasons and no alternative may agricultural land be used as a last option.

123298.explanation: Disagree: Brogum is important for culture and youth. VVD wants critical scrutiny of subsidies: money only with demonstrable social value and healthy finances. Stopping can happen if there is no support or structural deficit, but new initiatives should first be given a chance to become self-sufficient.

123304.explanation: Agree: This increases supply for starters but can also be used for co-housing and caregivers. On Schouwen-Duiveland this is possible, but often a housing formation permit is required and rules for safety and quality of life must be followed. This way, we use homes better without nuisance.

123305.explanation: Disagree: Making paid parking more expensive imposes extra burdens on residents and entrepreneurs. Parking fees must remain reasonable and not be used as a source of income.

123307.explanation: Disagree: The center is already largely car-reduced. Fully car-free goes too far, but car-reduced policy with good parking facilities at the edge and preferably underground is a good balance.

123308.explanation: Agree: We help owners make older homes more sustainable with advice, loans, and small subsidies where it saves a lot.

123310.explanation: Disagree: The dam belongs to Rijkswaterstaat. Residents of Schouwen-Duiveland must be able to continue parking for free.

123251.explanation: We have the possibility to do this

123292.explanation: If it benefits acceptance of tourism by residents and improves the tourist product

123250.explanation: People should be elected based on qualities and not on gender.

123251.explanation: We support a consultative referendum; this is not binding. The decision-making lies with the municipal council. This way, people can express their opinions on socially important topics.

123254.explanation: Every farm business is free to apply for a permit for this. When the plans comply with laws and regulations, the permit can be granted.

123257.explanation: We have voted for emergency shelter for 5 years for a maximum of 250 residents. We believe we must take responsibility in this matter.

123258.explanation: Housing must be safe and humane and comply with laws and regulations regarding housing. This is to prevent exploitation.

123262.explanation: This fosters mutual connection and can act preventively against illegal fireworks.

123263.explanation: The current waste processing system works fine, including the cards for the recycling center.

123266.explanation: Every farmer can apply for a permit from the municipality to build houses themselves. The application must be assessed against the applicable laws and regulations.

123267.explanation: There are many schemes within minimum income policy so that everyone can participate in sports.

123270.explanation: Parents who cannot afford this should be helped with it.

123274.explanation: We must all work together as a society to improve the feeling of safety.

123275.explanation: It works preventively and is a relatively inexpensive way to supervise a rural area.

123276.explanation: We do not oppose this negatively.

123280.explanation: This should be possible under certain conditions.

123283.explanation: Everyone who is unable to participate themselves must have sufficient means to live on.

123284.explanation: Our minimum income policy is generous; if it appears it is not generous enough, we can increase it.

123288.explanation: For the Christian Union, this is an important point.

123290.explanation: We are a financially healthy municipality and therefore there is no need to maintain a high property tax (ozb).

123292.explanation: This is done in consultation with the sector.

123294.explanation: A day of rest is good for everyone.

123295.explanation: We must meet the targets set by The Hague.

123297.explanation: Priority is given to placing solar panels on the roofs of business buildings and homes. Under conditions, it should also be possible to place solar panels on agricultural land provided it is well integrated into the landscape.

123298.explanation: The current subsidy should be sufficient.

123304.explanation: This can help counteract the housing shortage. However, housing must be safe and humane and comply with laws and regulations regarding housing. This is to prevent exploitation.

123305.explanation: This does not fit with the hospitable character of our island.

123307.explanation: Accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs is important.

123308.explanation: There is already a subsidy policy for this.

123310.explanation: This does not fit with the hospitable character of our island.

123250.explanation: For D66, the quality of the candidate aldermen nominated by the political parties is the most important. As far as D66 is concerned, these can all be female candidate aldermen.

123251.explanation: In a referendum, a complicated subject is simplified into a yes/no question, and citizens cannot explain their answers. We believe there are better ways to let residents express their opinions on important issues, such as surveys, citizen motions, and good participation processes.

123254.explanation: D66 supports farm shops because they shorten the food chain and the money goes directly to the farmer. And campsites on farms suit the character of Schouwen-Duiveland, provided they do not expand into holiday parks.

123257.explanation: Schouwen-Duiveland has a lot of space and few inhabitants. As far as D66 is concerned, we can therefore assist well with implementing the dispersion law. This relieves municipalities like Ter Apel. Additionally, our municipality does not have asylum reception but a temporary emergency shelter for five years.

123258.explanation: It is important that our agriculture and horticulture can provide food supply, which is not possible without seasonal workers. Employers have a responsibility for humane housing, but so does the municipality, especially to protect seasonal workers from abuses.

123262.explanation: We consider this an example of something society can organize well itself if there is a need. For example, in the form of a New Year's association. The municipality has a supportive and facilitating role here, through information provision and possibly subsidies.

123263.explanation: D66 wants to encourage people to think circularly, and allowing free dumping of bulky waste on the street does not help with that. Reuse and recycling are preferred. Just put that piece of furniture on the Bargain Corner or Marktplaats. Then it will also be picked up for free and will get a second life.

123266.explanation: D66 thinks it is important that more houses are built but also that we protect our unique polder landscape. Each situation must be assessed to see if these two can continue to coexist well and, if so, what is possible at that location.

123267.explanation: D66 believes this is not the municipality’s task but the sports clubs’ own, who can provide more tailored solutions. We prefer to use costly administrative capacity to support sports clubs and ensure all children have access to sports regardless of their parents' income.

123270.explanation: This already happens and we will continue because D66 finds music education very important. However, we believe this can be done more efficiently and cheaply. More focus could also be placed on linking to music associations, as that is where people play together and friendships are formed.

123274.explanation: Just like the mayor, who is responsible for safety in the municipality, D66 sees no reason to deploy more enforcement officers. In any case, we prefer prevention over repression. Therefore, D66 invests in youth and young people's workers and social networks, such as associations.

123275.explanation: D66 currently sees no reason for more camera surveillance. We ensure that unsafe places are made safe, possibly with temporary camera surveillance. This authority and responsibility lie with the mayor, and we as the municipal council faction will monitor him on this.

123276.explanation: Safety is the foundation for everything because if you are not safe, you cannot live a free life. For D66, there is no debate that everyone must feel safe. And if you feel unsafe, you should be able to seek help anonymously.

123280.explanation: D66 wants to make temporary living on holiday parks and recreational homes possible for island residents who urgently need housing (think of people going through a divorce).

123283.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes is an effective helping hand to give people living around the subsistence level more purchasing power. Additionally, it is important that other purchasing power schemes are better promoted without unnecessary bureaucracy.

123284.explanation: Our municipality already has a relatively generous policy for poverty support. Let’s first ensure that residents can use this without unnecessary bureaucracy. Furthermore, D66 aims to offer other things as cheaply as possible. For example, we want free swimming lessons.

123287.explanation: This is already the case in many places. If a traffic situation is unsafe, it must be adjusted. D66 finds it important that the municipality listens better to residents. This has not always gone well, such as with resident participation on traffic in the city center of Zierikzee.

123288.explanation: D66 trusts the professionalism of healthcare providers to deliver the best care, regardless of differences in faith, culture, or beliefs. Moreover, the municipality is only responsible for household care. All other personal care is arranged through health insurers.

123289.explanation: For D66, the quality of youth care is more important than the price. Additionally, care workers deserve a fair wage and, in our view, should not be disadvantaged by market forces resulting from municipalities seeking the cheapest options. Good care is not free.

123290.explanation: The property tax for commercial properties (such as shops and hospitality venues) and rental homes should be lowered, in our opinion. This allows owners (who are already facing VAT increases) to have money left to invest in their business or new rental homes. This benefits customers and housing seekers.

123292.explanation: Schouwen-Duiveland is a wealthy municipality, partly thanks to tourism. Moreover, tourists already pay more VAT under the current cabinet. Therefore, D66 sees no reason to tax tourists extra now. We prefer that they have money left to spend at local entrepreneurs.

123294.explanation: Residents have the freedom to observe Sunday rest, and entrepreneurs, in D66's view, have the freedom to open their stores on Sundays.

123295.explanation: As long as the asylum chain is stuck, priority is needed to get the flow going, and if it quiets down in the future, less priority needs to be given. For D66, this is not fixed and must always be determined based on waiting list data what is necessary and fair.

123297.explanation: As long as not all roofs are covered with solar panels, D66 thinks it is a shame to cover ground that characterizes our unique polder landscape and is needed for our food supply with solar panels.

123298.explanation: It is great for the appeal and liveliness of Schouwen-Duiveland that we are getting a full-fledged pop venue with Brogum. Cultural institutions deserve support from the municipality— not just Brogum but also, for example, the Nieuwe Kerk, fiZi, and Cultuurhuis ZierikC.

123304.explanation: D66 sees this as a good solution to help single housing seekers (such as starters, or people going through a divorce who urgently need housing) find a home. It is odd that a family of four is considered one household, but a group of four friends is not.

123305.explanation: But only in the high season. This way, we encourage tourists to use the free parking spaces and prevent searching traffic in Zierikzee, Renesse, and Burgh-Haamstede. Additionally, D66 opts for sustainable and accessible alternatives: good cycling facilities and free public transport in the high season.

123307.explanation: D66 finds it important that fewer cars come into the center. At the same time, we do not want it to be impossible for residents to access their homes by car. We therefore want to move towards a system with access gates so that tourists and residents can be distinguished.

123308.explanation: By making old homes more sustainable, we future-proof them. D66 finds it important to help residents who want to make their homes more sustainable but do not have sufficient means themselves.

123310.explanation: Free parking at the Brouwersdam makes a day at the beach accessible to residents, tourists, and water sports enthusiasts. D66 wants to keep it that way because everyone should be able to enjoy this hotspot.

123250.explanation: We find representation and diversity important. A women’s quota for the college of mayor and aldermen has no added value for us because choices may then feel artificial. Qualities come first, and those exist among women and non-binary persons too.

123251.explanation: By involving residents directly in important decisions, you increase support for policies and enhance engagement.

123254.explanation: Small-scale ancillary activities such as a small shop are fine, but no additional campsites or beds for tourists.

123257.explanation: We find it important that both the municipality and the COA adhere to the agreements already made.

123258.explanation: We are against housing seasonal workers by the employer. In practice, this often happens under poor conditions and at high costs. Due to dependency on the employer, an undesirable and vulnerable combination of work and housing arises.

123262.explanation: With the ban on consumer fireworks, a fireworks show combined with a New Year's Eve party would be a good way to celebrate this holiday.

123263.explanation: This prevents illegal dumping and keeps the neighborhood clean for everyone.

123266.explanation: Building must be planned and based on community needs (affordable), not for profit.

123267.explanation: Income should never be a barrier to being able to play sports or participate in clubs.

123270.explanation: Culture is for everyone, not just for children of parents with deep pockets.

123274.explanation: We prefer more neighborhood officers, but enforcement against nuisance may be necessary.

123275.explanation: We choose more police presence on the street instead of a surveillance state.

123276.explanation: Safety for vulnerable residents must be locally and immediately available.

123280.explanation: We support permanent residency in vacation homes in specific cases. Due to the housing shortage, this can be a solution for residents, for example, during a divorce. It is explicitly intended for residents and not for outsiders who buy a vacation home to live here.

123283.explanation: You cannot squeeze blood from a stone. This support is desperately needed against poverty.

123284.explanation: Working poor and lower middle incomes often find themselves squeezed now.

123287.explanation: For safety and livability in the streets.

123288.explanation: We trust that professionals in care do good work regardless of religion, culture, or beliefs. Care recipients must have confidence in the care provided.

123289.explanation: Here, quality and the child’s well-being must be central, not the lowest price.

123290.explanation: The SP supports tax relief for residents. We want to compensate for the loss of income by increasing the tourist tax.

123292.explanation: The benefits of tourism are unfairly distributed. Profits end up in the pockets of park managers; residents only suffer nuisances. The SP wants to raise the tourist tax and use the extra revenue to reduce taxes for residents.

123294.explanation: Shopkeepers should be allowed to decide for themselves on which days they open their store doors.

123295.explanation: We believe there must be enough social rental housing for everyone. Refugees with residence permits and local housing seekers should be able to live equally, without being played off against each other. Housing is a basic right.

123297.explanation: First cover all roofs with panels before sacrificing valuable land.

123298.explanation: Brogum is an essential cultural heart for young and old on the island.

123304.explanation: We must guard against absentee landlords and protect the livability of neighborhoods.

123305.explanation: This will drive up costs for our residents, so we disagree, unless residents can obtain a free parking permit allowing them to park anywhere in the municipality.

123307.explanation: For an atmospheric, safe, and healthy center where people are central instead of cars.

123308.explanation: The municipality must help residents lower their energy bills. Insulation is the solution.

123310.explanation: Tourists contribute to the maintenance of our nature reserves and beaches.

statements.title: Schouwen-Duiveland must introduce paid parking along the Brouwersdam.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The inhabitants of Schouwen-Duiveland must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Schouwen-Duiveland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Schouwen-Duiveland must establish a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Schouwen-Duiveland must deploy more municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There must be more camera surveillance in Schouwen-Duiveland.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The center of Zierikzee must become car-free.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Schouwen-Duiveland must stop funding Poppodium Brogum.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Paid parking in the town centers must become more expensive.

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: Schouwen-Duiveland must allocate more money to making old homes more sustainable.
